channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: I got a first look at the new Lego flower sets in a botanical garden, and the Mini Orchid should be on your list
item.description: Lego is celebrating its latest flower sets with a Botanical Garden pop-up, and we got to see the latest sets from the brand.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: DeepSeek AI App Withdrawn from Italian Stores Amid Data Concerns
item.description: Italian authorities request information on DeepSeek's data use, sparking concerns about personal data protection and AI manipulation.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Sam Bankman-Fried’s parents appealing to Trump to pardon their beloved son: report
item.description: The mother of Sam Bankman-Fried has apparently had a major change of heart about Trump after once leading a top Silicon Valley fundraising network for anti-Trump Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Trump vows sweeping tariffs on oil, pharmaceuticals and semiconductors
item.description: President Trump on Friday vowed to impose sweeping tariffs on semiconductor chips, pharmaceuticals, steel and aluminum and oil and gas. "We'll be doing pharmaceuticals. Importantly in drugs and medicines, etc. All forms of medicine and pharmaceuticals," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Capital One’s new AI agent will help you buy your next car
item.description: Capital One has launched an AI agent designed to help consumers with one of the most frustrating, time-consuming processes in life: buying a car. The banking giant’s Chat Concierge provides information, makes decisions, and takes action using multiple AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: NFL officials spotting the football isn't going away despite several technological advances
item.description: The NFL will engage its Competition Committee on technology to take virtual line-to-gain measurements next season, but officials will continue to spot the football.
item.dc:creator: Rob Maaddi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5G connectivity on your smart ring or Meta's future Ray-Bans? This eSIM solution smaller than a grain of rice makes it possible and could pave the way to an 'app store' of networks
item.description: The world’s smallest GSMA-compliant eSIM is smaller than a grain of rice and reduces PCB space by 37 times compared to Nano SIMs.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Report: Apple is stopping work on a pair of smart glasses that would have connected to the Mac
item.description: Apple’s reported work on a pair of AR smart glasses that would have connected to a Mac for power has been canceled or shelved this week, according to new reporting from Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: World's first AI-designed, 3D-printed shoe wants to be the next Crocs
item.description: A startup claims to have developed the world's first entirely artificial intelligence-designed and 3D-printed shoe, the brainchild of a 25-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tesla promises more affordable EVs and could start its Robotaxi service in June – but I've heard this all before
item.description: Elon Musk makes more big Tesla promises during a quarterly earnings call, but I'd like to see it go back to doing what it does best.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump to meet with CEO of chipmaker Nvidia
item.description: President Trump will meet with the CEO of chipmaking powerhouse Nvidia on Friday as the White House reportedly mulls tightening chip exports to China, a White House official confirmed to The Hill. The meeting with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang comes as competition between the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The Rise and Fall of DeepSeek: Italian Authority Blocks Chinese AI App
item.description: Italy's data protection authority Garante blocks popular chatbot service DeepSeek, citing concerns over user data privacy and transparency.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: UPS is hugely scaling back its Amazon shipping deal
item.description: UPS has confirmed plans to reduce its contract with Amazon by more than 50% by the second half of 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Banco Sabadell evoluciona la experiencia digital de sus clientes
item.description: En los últimos años, a pesar de las estrictas regulaciones y el carácter sensible del sector, las organizaciones dedicadas a la banca y finanzas han dado pasos en firme en materia de transformación digital y adopción de nuevas tecnologías con el fin de mejorar la operativa del negocio y la experienc
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Plunge into Python: New tools and tips for Python developers
item.description: Create luscious-looking full-stack websites in nothing but Python, get a hands-on lesson in integrating Rust and Python, and learn everything you need to know about the care and feeding of Python virtual environments.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Workforce collaboration strategies to keep your teams happy
item.description: No matter where employees work day-to-day, the office is the heart of every business. It’s where companies create and reinforce the culture and experience that are central to their relationship with employees.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: HP redefines PC refresh approach using performance data
item.description: Traditionally, a PC refresh plan is scheduled based on the age of the devices, but as technology advances, so do opportunities. PCs today often continue to perform as needed for much longer than established in a lifecycle policy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Bringing zero downtime to anywhere work
item.description: In a work-from-anywhere world, how can a team of so few be expected to keep doing so much for so many? The pace is unsustainable. It’s time for IT to enlist some support of its own and delegate—handing off time-consuming tasks to people and to intelligent, automated tools.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How Apple Vision Pro is finding a home in healthcare
item.description: In early 2024, Ryan Broderick, a surgeon at UC San Diego Health, was commiserating with some colleagues about the profusion of screens in today’s operating rooms. Though the displays provided essential guidance for minimally invasive operations in progress, they also added complexity.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft AI investments cause cloud operating income growth to plunge
item.description: Microsoft reported strong earnings on Wednesday, but growth in profits in its intelligent cloud segment is slowing despite booming demand for AI infrastructure. It reported revenue of $25.5 billion in its intelligent cloud segment, around 37% of its total revenue, in the three months to Dec.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Timeline of HPE’s $14 billion bid for Juniper
item.description: AI is a key factor in one of the tech industry’s most significant M&A developments: HPE’s proposed acquisition of Juniper Networks. The $14 billion deal would shake up the industry, and regulatory watchdogs in the U.S. and worldwide have raised antitrust concerns.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI-powered automation set for gains in 2025
item.description: Automation technology has long been a part of IT’s arsenal, as enterprises work to simplify increasingly complex networking and operational environments. Looking ahead, the more AI is added to the automation mix, the more enterprise operations teams stand to gain.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week? That's what ChatGPT maker OpenAI is suggesting, along with U.S. President Donald Trump's top AI adviser.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: DeepSeek underscores US-China race for AI dominance, OpenAI says
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The AI bust is here
item.description: Hands up! Who remembers the dot.com bust? I do. It was March 10, 2000. The NASDAQ Composite index peaked at 5,048.62 points. That doesn’t sound like much today, but it was up 800% from 1995. Then people started to get nervous about the true value of the dot.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The biggest ideas in software and technology today
item.description: One of the coolest things about being a software developer is that you often know what’s behind the curtain. To some extent, at least.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: UK regulator may investigate AWS and Microsoft cloud businesses
item.description: AWS and Microsoft could potentially face an investigation of their cloud businesses in Britain should the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) follow the recommendation of an independent inquiry group that reports into the UK’s antitrust regulator.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Unknown threat actor targeting Juniper routers with backdoor: Report
item.description: Network administrators using routers from Juniper Networks are being urged to scan for possible compromise after the discovery that an unknown threat actor has been installing a backdoor in customer routers since at least 2023.
item.dc:creator: Network World
